title: Local Terminology Applicability Profile
description: A local site's ASSERTED terminology contract: which code systems and versions it speaks, how its local codes map to standard codes, which result-status lifecycle states may inform a decision, and what an observation must carry before it can be interpreted. Terminology applicability cannot be inferred — an unversioned code system, an unmapped local code, or an ambiguous result status means the observation is not interpretable, not that it is interpretable by default. `preliminary`, `corrected`, and `amended` are first-class lifecycle states here, not annotations: a preliminary result is not a final result, and a lost or ambiguous corrected/amended state is a hard failure rather than a warning, because acting on a superseded value is precisely the harm this contract exists to prevent. Structural validity NEVER implies clinical validity or local applicability. NOTE ON THE STATUS VALUE SET: it is bound to an explicit `resourceType`. Observation.status and DiagnosticReport.status are DIFFERENT value sets, and the earlier unbound list mixed them — `partial` and `appended` are DiagnosticReport statuses and are not valid Observation statuses at all.

profileClass.description: `synthetic_example` is structurally forbidden from carrying authority by this schema's top-level conditional and can never satisfy an activation gate.

codeSystems.description: Code systems this site asserts it speaks, each pinned to an exact version. An observation carrying a code system version this profile does not assert is not interpretable.

localMappings.description: Local code to standard code mappings. A local code with no entry here is UNMAPPED — the observation fails closed and stays visible. An empty array is legal and means nothing is mapped; it does not mean everything passes through. Two entries for the same localCode + localSystem that disagree are a MAPPING_CONFLICT and are never resolved by declaration order.

assertion.description: Three-state discriminator. Only `asserted` can contribute to applicability; `unknown` and `not_supplied` fail closed and remain visible. No 'assume default' state exists. When a container is `asserted`, EVERY value in that container must be non-null — a null secondary value is not a wildcard.

observationStatus.description: FHIR R4/R5 ObservationStatus, complete and exact. `partial` and `appended` are DiagnosticReport statuses and are deliberately absent: an Observation cannot legally carry them, so permitting them let a site configure acceptance of a status its results can never have.

informaticsOwnerName.description: OWNER-HELD (plan OQ-3). No repository agent may populate this field.

then.description: An assertion by nobody is not an assertion. `asserted` with every identity field still null was previously valid.

attachmentContract.description: PLACEHOLDER — bound to the P2 attachment contract. The signature/attestation envelope (signer identity, verification, revocation) is out of scope here and lands with P2's authenticated-attachment primitive so the two do not diverge.

signatureState.description: Unsigned profiles fail closed today: `not_executed_owner_held` can never satisfy an activation gate. `bound` additionally requires a non-null attachmentRef, and is refused by `evaluateActivationGate` regardless because no attachment verifier exists on this side.

supersededBy.description: Must be non-null whenever reviewState is `superseded`. A dangling successor pointer is a hard failure.

reviewIntervalDays.description: Null is DELIBERATELY still permitted: it is a representable unknown that fails closed at evaluation time as REVIEW_CADENCE_UNKNOWN.

version.description: Exact code-system version. Null with assertion `unknown` is representable and fails closed; it is never treated as 'current'.

resultStatusPolicy.description: Which observation lifecycle states this site recognizes and which may inform a decision. `preliminary`, `corrected`, and `amended` are first-class states: a preliminary result treated as final, or a corrected/amended result whose lineage is missing, must fail closed. The required memberships stated below are ENFORCED by `contains` constraints, not asserted in prose.

resourceType.description: The FHIR resource type this status value set is bound to. A status value set is only meaningful bound to a resource type: Observation.status and DiagnosticReport.status are different value sets and were previously conflated. Only Observation is supported in this phase; a DiagnosticReport-level profile is a separate contract (charter §2.6a, owner-held).

recognizedStates.description: Must include `preliminary`, `corrected`, `amended`, `entered-in-error`, `cancelled`, and `unknown`. A profile that does not recognize a state cannot safely receive observations carrying it.

acceptedForDecision.description: Statuses that may inform a decision. `preliminary` is deliberately not permissible here by construction, and `appended` has been removed because it is not an Observation status at all. Including `corrected`/`amended` is only meaningful together with requireStatusLineage true, so the superseding relationship is proven rather than assumed.

blockingStates.description: Statuses that must fail closed and stay visible. Must include `preliminary`, `registered`, `unknown`, `entered-in-error`, and `cancelled` — now enforced. Note that `entered-in-error` and `cancelled` additionally receive their own distinct blocker codes at evaluation time, because a retraction and a never-performed test demand different actions from a human than a merely non-decision-grade status.

requireStatusLineage.description: Always true. The site must be able to prove, for every observation, that its status lineage is complete — i.e. that no correction or amendment has been dropped in transit. This is a const rather than a boolean because a profile that opts out of lineage proof cannot detect a dropped correction at all.

requireEffectiveTime.description: Clinically meaningful time of observation. Always required; a result with no effective time cannot be age-banded.

requireIssuedTime.description: Time the result was released. Required to detect a later correction.

maxResultAgeDays.description: Beyond this age a result is stale for decision purposes. Null is DELIBERATELY still permitted: it is a representable unknown that fails closed at evaluation time as STALENESS_POLICY_UNKNOWN.

unitCode.description: Unit the mapped local code reports in, intended to be a UCUM code (docs/clinical/local-profile-charter-contract.md item 15/15a) but held here as an opaque, unvalidated string — nothing checks that the value is valid UCUM syntax (owner-held gap, see local-profile-charter-contract.md §2.7 C13). What IS enforced: it must be non-null on an asserted mapping (a null unit was previously truthiness-guarded on both sides and became a silent wildcard), and an observation whose unit string differs fails closed on exact-string mismatch; it is never parsed, normalized, or converted.

specimenCode.description: Must be non-null on an asserted mapping, for the same reason as unitCode.

mappingVersion.description: The mapping carries its own version, independent of the profile version, because a mapping can be revised without the surrounding profile changing.

mappingAuthority.description: Named authority accountable for this mapping. OWNER-HELD (plan OQ-3).

equivalence.description: Only `equivalent` supports decision use. `wider`, `narrower`, and `inexact` are representable so they stay visible, and they fail closed rather than being rounded to `equivalent`.
